titles.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for the One Who Consumes Carrion, Blood, and Pork

text: 5133 - رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "آكِلُ اَلْمَيْتَةِ وَ اَلدَّمِ وَ لَحْمِ اَلْخِنْزِيرِ عَلَيْهِ أَدَبٌ فَإِنْ عَادَ أُدِّبَ" قُلْتُ فَإِنْ عَادَ قَالَ "يُؤَدَّبُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَتْلٌ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5133 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"One who consumes carrion, blood, or pork is to be disciplined. If he returns to it, he is disciplined again."
I asked: "And if he does it again?"
Imam (as) replied: "He is to be disciplined, but there is no execution upon him."
